From my side, there is no tax wrinkle behind the stale alert.\\n\\nRyan can treat this as fully closed from a tax review standpoint.\\n\\nThanks,\\nHannah\", \"cc\": [\"ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"alex.cahoon@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-04-21T20:05:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"18da6bdab650491d81ed75452021d3ba\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"anaya.patel@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Final status check on filing calendar exception\", \"content\": \"Hi Peter,\\n\\nFollowing up on the filing calendar cleanup, I have the Northstar Legal annual report reminder and one other exception ready for final review. If the BlackLine activity log surfaces with the client, I will handle any client-facing explanation and keep the wording anchored to the polling auto-fire point rather than anything that reads like renewed review activity.\\n\\nPlease leave the accounting system as-is; the priority here is a clean audit trail, not any further update.\\n\\nBest,\\nMatthew\", \"cc\": [\"james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-24T17:45:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0007\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f\", \"content\": \"Hi Harry,\\n\\nConfirmed from my side that the August 7 closure still stands for the Northstar Legal July exception on account 225000. The stale auto-fire does not indicate any actual review work restarted on the item; it looks like reminder noise against the already closed case tied to the $380.50 timing difference and reconciliation BL-5FEFEA879504.\\n\\nNo substantive work reopened the case, and there is nothing further to push through the accounting records based on this alert.\\n\\nThanks,\\nJames\", \"cc\": [\"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-24T17:43:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}]"}

The CrownPeak credit-memo backfill is non-negotiable for bookkeeping close, so please keep that on the follow-through list until Brenda has it in hand. Monday 2026-06-08 at 14:00 works for me. Please send the invite to me, you, Owen, Brenda, Anaya, and Priya so we can align on both the CrownPeak cleanup and whether that cancellation-event cross-check needs to become a standing control.

Once the journal entry is posted and the updated balances are reflected in the final file, I will countersign the final deliverable.

Best,
Andrea", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_063_annual_accounts_prep_northstar_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-19T14:22:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0002", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Draft Acme Cloud change-order proposal for partner review", "content": "Hi George,

Both scope lines are valid to take forward, and I'm comfortable with the proposal direction.

Before this goes to the client, please make two revisions:

First, separate the pricing for the sales-tax work into a quarterly nexus-check fixed fee and a distinct monthly state-coding hourly rate. I do not want those blended into one scope line.

Second, reframe the AR-aging bucket cleanup hourly cap to align with the benchmark language we used on the prior Northstar engagement. Keep the cap defensible and positioned as comparable to that precedent.

Once the revised draft is ready to send, please hold a 30-minute contingency slot with the Acme CFO for shortly after the proposal lands in case they want to discuss live.

Please turn the revision quickly and send me the clean version before it goes out.

Best,
Andrea", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-15T14:20:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "e6d50c7359844e17af5b114da08a60b4", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com", "peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Status check: VEN-004-159840 audit watchpoints before Friday close", "content": "Hi James,

Thank you for sending those specifics. I have added VEN-004-159840 and JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0001 to the weekly risk scan, and I am also carrying forward exc_abd8e711f5f347 so we have a clear record of the approval dependency. Please let me know if its status changes before Monday.

Best,
Marina", "cc": [], "parent_id": "8eed093bd9154a429ee06d069620c003", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-08T19:08:23Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0007", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["william.white@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Documenting stale SLA dismissal follow-up for exc_344bcd6e4c7a4c", "content": "Hi William,

I reviewed the tax angle on the $2,267.83 FX revaluation tied to Northstar Legal account 103000 Cash - Tax Reserve in northstar_legal_FP-2025-09.

Nothing on the federal tax side is triggered here. At that amount, on a single-period revaluation, there is no reporting threshold issue and no separate tax-treatment concern that would require us to reopen the prior conclusion.

The FY-2025 GBP cash-tax-reserve translation also ties cleanly to the FP-2025-09 close commentary Alex referenced. From my side, there is no tax wrinkle behind the stale alert.

Ryan can treat this as fully closed from a tax review standpoint.

Thanks,
Hannah", "cc": ["ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com", "alex.cahoon@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-04-21T20:05:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "18da6bdab650491d81ed75452021d3ba", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "anaya.patel@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Final status check on filing calendar exception", "content": "Hi Peter,

Following up on the filing calendar cleanup, I have the Northstar Legal annual report reminder and one other exception ready for final review. The Northstar item has already been shifted to the updated due date, and the remaining entry appears to be a documentation follow-up rather than an active deadline issue. If you agree, I will mark the tracker accordingly and close out the open items.

Best,
Anaya", "cc": [], "parent_id": "985dfd0c4fa44f1da5d669e437aa101c", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-27T13:39:27Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0008", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f", "content": "Hi Harry,

I have filed this message under the Northstar engagement folder. If the BlackLine activity log surfaces with the client, I will handle any client-facing explanation and keep the wording anchored to the polling auto-fire point rather than anything that reads like renewed review activity.

Please leave the accounting system as-is; the priority here is a clean audit trail, not any further update.

Best,
Matthew", "cc": ["james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com", "jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-24T17:45:00Z", "is_read": false}, {"email_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0007", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f", "content": "Hi Harry,

Confirmed from my side that the August 7 closure still stands for the Northstar Legal July exception on account 225000. The stale auto-fire does not indicate any actual review work restarted on the item; it looks like reminder noise against the already closed case tied to the $380.50 timing difference and reconciliation BL-5FEFEA879504.

No substantive work reopened the case, and there is nothing further to push through the accounting records based on this alert.

Thanks,
James", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-24T17:43:00Z", "is_read": true}]

[{"id": "issue_70fdb3e2b126483d9d6d8b8d39eaf48a", "project_id": "linear_63697b7dff5c", "team_id": "linear_5477c0808ac2", "parent_id": null, "assignee_id": "npc_029", "state_id": "done", "cycle_id": null, "number": null, "title": "Close out CrownPeak three-item follow-up arc on existing record", "description": "Append closeout note to existing CrownPeak follow-up issue.

Third item summary: apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 / VEN-030-736427 for $422.32 on northstar_legal account 219000 is not a duplicate. Manual review tied the line memo to a Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer pre-bill that was cancelled by mutual agreement on 2026-02-28 when northstar_legal moved IR coverage in-house. The pre-bill was issued on 2026-03-11 (now 81 days old in pending_approval) about eleven days after the cancellation was already on record, and it stayed in pending_approval because the original pre-bill was never voided and no credit memo was issued at cancellation.

Cross-scenario consolidation for the three CrownPeak invoices: this existing issue is the single breadcrumb for the full follow-through arc, including Owen's stale-void recommendation, Brenda's confirmation that CrownPeak AR has the cancellation on record and will issue a manual credit memo, Tariq's line-level detail pull, and Daniel's direction to treat the item as stale and close the three-of-three queue here.

Process note from Anaya Wallace, with Priya Khatri owning the AP-side policy framing: add a recurring cross-check between vendor cancellation events and AP invoices still sitting in pending_approval so cancelled retainers and similar pre-bills are surfaced before they age. input.command:
cd /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/063c145d-f463-4305-adc1-8ab65fff7dcf/tool-results/ 2>/dev/null; python3 -c "
import json,datetime
d=json.load(open('mcp-mcp_34e68b70-sap_subledger_list_ap_invoices-1781857381065.txt'))
r=json.loads(d['result'])
inv=r['ap_invoices']
today=datetime.date(2026,6,12)
print('returned',len(inv),'of total',r.get('total_ap_invoices'))
pend=[i for i in inv if i.get('status')=='pending_approval']
print('pending_approval in this batch:',len(pend))
for i in sorted(pend,key=lambda x:x.get('invoice_date') or ''):
    dt=(i.get('invoice_date') or '')[:10]
    try:
        age=(today-datetime.date.fromisoformat(dt)).days
    except: age='?'
    print(f\"{age}d  {i['id']}  {i.get('vendor_name')}  {i.get('invoice_number')}  \${i.get('amount')}  acct{i.get('gl_account_number')}  inv={dt}\")
"

content.text: Let me parse the saved AP and journal-entry files for complete aging and the May activity.

input.command:
cd /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/063c145d-f463-4305-adc1-8ab65fff7dcf/tool-results/ 2>/dev/null
echo "===== PENDING AP (all northstar) ====="
python3 -c "
import json,datetime
d=json.load(open('mcp-mcp_34e68b70-sap_subledger_list_ap_invoices-1781857513752.txt'))
r=json.loads(d['result'])
inv=r['ap_invoices']
today=datetime.date(2026,6,12)
print('pending total returned:',len(inv),'declared total:',r.get('total_ap_invoices'))
tot=0
rows=[]
for i in inv:
    dt=(i.get('invoice_date') or '')[:10]
    try: age=(today-datetime.date.fromisoformat(dt)).days
    except: age=-1
    rows.append((age,i))
    tot+=i.get('amount',0)
for age,i in sorted(rows,key=lambda x:-x[0]):
    flag=' <<< >60d' if age>60 else ''
    print(f\"{age:4}d  {i['id']}  {i.get('vendor_name')[:28]:28}  {i.get('invoice_number')}  \${i.get('amount'):>9}  acct{i.get('gl_account_number')}{flag}\")
print('TOTAL pending amount: \$%.2f across %d invoices'%(tot,len(inv)))
over60=[i for a,i in rows if a>60]
print('Over 60 days:',len(over60),'amount \$%.2f'%sum(i['amount'] for i in over60))
"

    amt=j.get('total_debit') or j.get('amount')
    print(j.get('id'), '|', j.get('status'),'|',j.get('entry_type'),'|\$',amt,'|',desc)
" 2>&1 | head -60

input.content:
# Northstar Legal — Engagement Status Review
**Prepared by:** George McAdam (Accounts Senior) · **For:** Daniel Jones (ahead of partner discussion with Matthew Li)
**Date:** 2026-06-12 · **Client/entity:** northstar_legal · **Basis:** northstar_legal GL through FP-2026-05 close (5/31/2026), SAP sub-ledger, Records Vault, Linear, Airtable, Slack/email (last ~10 weeks)

---

## 1. Bottom line
The Northstar work is structurally messy in three distinct places, and the comms trail only captures a small slice of it:

1. **Scope is undocumented.** The only engagement letter on file in Records Vault for Northstar is the **FY2026 *Audit* Engagement Letter (executed 2026-05-11)**. There is **no accounting/bookkeeping or tax/advisory engagement letter on file** for the recurring work we are actually performing and recognizing revenue/WIP against. This is the single biggest exposure for the partner meeting.
2. **AP approval backlog is large and aged** — far bigger than the 3 CrownPeak items the team has been discussing. **87 invoices, $302,063 total, sit in pending_approval on Northstar's books; 82 of them ($288,176) are >60 days old, with a long tail running 200–339 days.**
3. **WIP is growing and not fully tied out / approved.** WIP (acct 119000) is **$2,013,943** and climbing (+$103,165 in May); the May WIP-recognition JEs are still **submitted, not posted**, and the **May close is past its BD5 deadline (6/5) but still open**.

My recommendation: this is not a clean "absorb it" situation. The accounting/tax/advisory scope needs to be papered (engagement letter or change order) before more work accrues, and the AP/WIP/close hygiene needs an owner. Detail below so you can decide absorb vs. change order with Matthew.

---

## 2. What the engagement letter covers vs. what we've actually done
**On file (Records Vault, entity northstar_legal):**
- `doc_0036f5b991574808` — **"Northstar Legal FY2026 Audit Engagement Letter — Executed 2026-05-11"** (classification: restricted; retention: FIRM_INTERNAL). *This is the only engagement_letter document on file (total engagement letters = 1).*
- Supporting FY2026 audit docs: Materiality memo (initial 2026-05-18).

**Work actually performed / delivered (evidenced in GL, vault, comms):**
- **Monthly bookkeeping & management accounts** — e.g. FP-2026-04 Management Accounts Package (filed 2026-05-07); ongoing monthly close (FP-2026-01 → 05).
- **FY2025 annual accounts** — working file tied to FP-2025-12, four support schedules, partner-draw ASC-710 reclass ($18,750) posted; final deliverable signed into the vault (Andrea Phil countersign).
- **FY2025 federal + state tax prep & filing** — Form 1065, K-1 allocations, M-1 recon; tax-prep data package (2026-06-08); SALT late-post of $4,820.30 authorized by William White; e-file pending client signature.
- **Tax advisory** — partner effective-rate modeling (~$180K aggregate savings scenario, Q4 cadence shift).

**Conclusion:** We are delivering full-scope outsourced accounting + tax + advisory, but the documented FY2026 engagement is **audit only**. Either an accounting/tax engagement letter exists and was never filed (documentation gap), or the recurring work is being performed without a current signed scope. Note also Andrea's 2026-05-15 email referenced *"the benchmark language we used on the prior Northstar engagement"* for an AR-aging cap on Acme — implying a Northstar change-order precedent exists historically, but nothing current is in the vault. **This needs to be resolved before the audit field-work deepens** (also relevant to independence/self-review, since we keep the books and would be auditing them).

---

## 3. Accounts receivable — outstanding, aging, stale risk *(northstar_legal books we maintain)*
- **AR – Client Billings (110000): $647,359** as of 5/31 close.
- **Allowance for Doubtful Accounts (117000): $1,057,728** — **the allowance EXCEEDS gross AR.** Net AR is effectively negative on the face of the ledger, which is not a sustainable position and signals either over-reserving or write-offs not yet applied against the allowance.
- **Bad Debt Expense (590000): $427,019 YTD.**
- **Deferred revenue / client retainers (240000): $2,043,052.**

---

## 4. WIP — does it tie out to invoiced & approved?

Root causes already identified in comms include orphaned-approver routing (departed staff) and miscoding — but those were diagnosed against isolated items, while the aged population is ~$288K.

## 6. Comms vs. the numbers — do they match?
**Mostly no.** The Slack/email trail over the last ~10 weeks reads as "routine cleanup, largely resolved." The numbers say otherwise:

content.text: Now the email to George and Daniel with full detail:

input.subject: Northstar engagement — full status ahead of the Matthew meeting (AR / WIP / AP / scope)
input.content:
Daniel,

Per your ask this morning, here's where the Northstar engagement actually stands across the books, the comms, and the engagement records. Full memo with supporting detail is filed in Records Vault (doc_fc9476b21dd4, restricted, 7-yr AICPA_SQMS) and a tracking record is logged in Airtable (AP Workflow Exceptions). Figures are northstar_legal GL through the FP-2026-05 close (5/31/26).

THE THREE THINGS THAT MATTER

1) SCOPE IS UNDOCUMENTED — the biggest exposure for your meeting.
The only engagement letter on file for Northstar is the FY2026 *Audit* Engagement Letter (executed 2026-05-11). There is NO accounting/bookkeeping or tax/advisory engagement letter on file, yet we're delivering all of it: monthly bookkeeping & management accounts, the FY2025 annual accounts (signed), FY2025 federal+state tax prep & filing (1065/K-1/M-1; SALT post authorized; e-file pending client signature), and tax advisory (the ~$180K partner effective-rate model). Either an accounting/tax letter exists and was never filed, or we're working beyond a documented scope. Andrea's 5/15 email even references "the benchmark language we used on the prior Northstar engagement," so a change-order precedent exists historically — but nothing current is in the vault. This also has an independence/self-review angle since we keep the books and are now the auditor.

2) AP APPROVAL BACKLOG is far bigger than the CrownPeak chatter suggests.
87 invoices ($302,063) sit in pending_approval on Northstar's books; 82 of them ($288,176) are over 60 days old, with a long tail running 200–339 days. Largest aged items: Stonepath $16,214 (137d), VaultSpan $12,896 (250d), ArchiveSpring $11,787 (130d), BlueMesa $10,998 (324d), Cobalt Harbor $10,867 (96d), Northbar $10,300 (208d), LexHarbor $9,864 (325d). The 3 CrownPeak items the team has been working (recode VEN-030-817856; dismiss duplicate VEN-030-353041; stale-void VEN-030-736427) all have agreed dispositions but are NOT yet executed in SAP. Root causes already surfaced (orphaned-approver routing from departed staff, miscoding) were only diagnosed against isolated items — the aged population is ~$288K.

3) WIP IS GROWING AND NOT TIED OUT.
WIP-Unbilled Time (119000) is $2,013,943, up $103,165 in May, and ~3x gross AR ($647K). The two May WIP-recognition JEs ($45,263 + $15,531) are still SUBMITTED, not posted — held pending the billable-hours tie-out per the Slack thread — and the May close (BD5 was 6/5) is still open. So WIP does not currently tie out to invoiced-and-approved; we're recognizing/billing slower than we're working.

AR / STALE RISK
AR (110000) is $647,359 and turns over monthly (~$300–370K billings and ~$300K+ collections per month), so the gross balance isn't heavily stale on its face. The real AR concern is that the Allowance for Doubtful Accounts (117000) is $1,057,728 — it EXCEEDS gross AR — alongside $427,019 bad-debt expense YTD. That points to collectability problems already being recognized and needs a deliberate reserve/write-off review. (Note: I couldn't produce a precise invoice-level day-bucket aging from the sub-ledger — recommend pulling a true aged-AR if you want exact buckets for the meeting.)

DO THE COMMS MATCH THE NUMBERS? Mostly no.
The Slack/email trail of the last ~10 weeks reads as "routine cleanup, largely resolved" — 3 CrownPeak items, FY2025 accounts done, FY2025 tax done, stale BlackLine re-fires dismissed. The numbers tell a different story: the ~$288K aged-AP backlog, the $2.0M WIP with unposted recognition JEs, the allowance > gross AR, the late/open May close, and the missing accounting/tax/advisory engagement letter are not in the comms at all.

OPEN ITEMS STILL TRACKING
Linear: 5 open issues, all CrownPeak/AP-specific (issue_0ab11dc6…, issue_ba662bde…, issue_dfa400bc…, issue_05de9a8f…, issue_7e7797e4…). None track the broader backlog, WIP, or scope gap.
Airtable: no Northstar engagement record existed; I created one in AP Workflow Exceptions (rec4683d261d08f40) pointing to the vault memo.

RECOMMENDATION (absorb vs. change order)
This isn't a clean "absorb it." My read: (1) paper the accounting/tax/advisory scope first — engagement letter or change order, using the prior-Northstar benchmark — before audit field-work deepens; (2) put the realization gap ($2.0M WIP vs $647K AR + unbilled May time) to Matthew as the change-order figure if the over-baseline effort is recurring; (3) assign an owner to clear the 82 aged AP items, starting with the 3 already-approved CrownPeak dispositions; (4) finish the May close; (5) revisit the AR reserve.

Happy to pull the aged-AR detail or break out the WIP/billing reconciliation further before you sit down with Matthew.

George

content.text:
Done. Here's what I found and what I've delivered.

## Where the Northstar engagement actually stands

I reviewed northstar_legal across Oracle GL (through the FP-2026-05 close), SAP sub-ledger, Records Vault, Linear, Airtable, and ~10 weeks of Slack/email. Three things matter for Daniel's meeting with Matthew:

**1. Scope is undocumented (biggest exposure).** The *only* engagement letter on file is the **FY2026 Audit Engagement Letter (executed 2026-05-11)**. There's no accounting/bookkeeping or tax/advisory letter, yet we're delivering all of it — monthly bookkeeping & management accounts, FY2025 annual accounts (signed), FY2025 1065/K-1/M-1 tax filing (e-file pending client signature), and tax advisory (~$180K partner-rate model). Andrea's 5/15 email even cites "the benchmark language we used on the prior Northstar engagement," so a change-order precedent existed historically — but nothing current is in the vault. Also an independence/self-review angle since we keep the books and now audit them.

**2. AP backlog is far bigger than the CrownPeak chatter.** 87 invoices ($302,063) in pending_approval on Northstar's books; **82 ($288,176) are >60 days old**, tail running 200–339 days (Stonepath $16K/137d, VaultSpan $12.9K/250d, BlueMesa $11K/324d, LexHarbor $9.9K/325d…). The 3 CrownPeak items the team discussed have agreed dispositions but **aren't executed in SAP yet**.

**3. WIP not tied out.** WIP (119000) = **$2,013,943, +$103K in May, ~3x gross AR**. The two May WIP-recognition JEs are still *submitted/unposted* (held for the hours tie-out), and the **May close is past BD5 (6/5) and still open**.

**AR:** $647,359, turning over monthly (so not heavily stale), but the **allowance ($1,057,728) exceeds gross AR** plus $427K bad-debt YTD — a reserve/collectability flag, not a simple staleness one.

**Comms vs numbers:** mostly a mismatch — the comms read "routine, resolved"; the aged-AP backlog, WIP, allowance anomaly, late close, and the missing engagement letter aren't in them at all.

## Deliverables completed
- **Records Vault:** filed the full memo — `doc_fc9476b21dd4`, kind `memo`, classification **restricted**, retention **AICPA_SQMS_7Y** (7-yr), entity northstar_legal (v2 holds the complete 11.4 KB body).
- **Airtable:** created engagement-status record `rec4683d261d08f40` in *AP Workflow Exceptions* (High/Open, owner George McAdam) summarizing the backlog + scope/WIP/AR flags and pointing to the memo.
- **Email:** sent to george.mcadam@ and daniel.jones@ (`bc1dd4fa…`) with the full breakdown and an absorb-vs-change-order recommendation.

One caveat I flagged in the memo and email: the sub-ledger gives AR at the summary level (no invoice-level day buckets), and Brookfield-side engagement time isn't separately tracked in these systems — the WIP figures are the WIP carried on Northstar's ledger that we recognize. If Daniel wants exact AR aging buckets before the meeting, I can pull a true aged-AR detail.
